reasoning: The effective yield of the ICE BofA 7-10 Year US Corporate Index stood at 4.96% on Jan 16, 2026. Data from the FRED statistical tool indicates a very slight downward linear trend (-0.000166 per day) over the last 30 business days and puts the prediction for Jan 25 at 4.931. Furthermore, 4.96% is near the top of the 4.90%-4.97% trading band established in January 2026 so far. A reversion to the mean of this band (around 4.93-4.94) is the most probable outcome for the near-term Jan 25 resolution date, though seasonality (yields have historically been solid in late Jan) and a recent small pop in US Treasury yields partially work against this. For the longer-term Feb 17 date, the uncertainty broadens, pushing my estimate closer to 0.50, especially given that Wall Street sees a rate cut in early 2026 as unlikely. Thus, I assign 44% for Jan 25 (expecting a slight drop to mean) and 47% for Feb 17.
